cases.focus: GET /wp/v2/settings emits JSON data, status, REST headers, and matched endpoint output
POST /wp/v2/settings reads body params, updates deterministic option storage, and emits refreshed JSON
unknown route emits REST error JSON and 404 status
HEAD request runs dispatch/status/header path and returns no body
rest_pre_serve_request can manually serve output and suppress default JSON emission

native_boundaries.reason: serve_request() uses PHP-native header/status/output effects. The probe captures these through a subclass override while preserving the WordPress-facing methods.
The settings endpoint runs through real REST/settings/option code, but deterministic wpdb storage replaces a live database for this HTTP-serving gate.
This gate executes serve_request() in PHP CLI processes. Full web-server/browser REST behavior remains an E2E follow-up.

remaining_gaps.detail: This gate captures serve_request() output/status/header intent in CLI. Web-server transport, real headers, REST URL routing, and browser fetch behavior remain later E2E closure.
Capability and logged-in state are deterministic PHP boundaries here. Full roles/users/authentication behavior remains outside this REST server gate.
The package owns the class file and dispatch decisions, but most WP_REST_Server method bodies still come from mirrored WordPress PHP source.
